reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C (isoxazole ring formation via [3+2])
bond_broken: N-O (partial, nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate via dehydration)

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are premixed in DES. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide is generated in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. No incompatibility between reagents at room temperature before heating. Single feed stream through heated coil is simplest and ensures immediate trapping.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure complete conversion of phenylacetylene

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; may act as mild promoter for nitrile oxide generation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; 1 mL total DES used for 0.5 mmol scale

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ, promoted by the DES environment at 120°C
Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form the isoxazole ring, giving ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively

stream_logic.reasoning: All components premixed in a single DES solution. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are both stable at room temperature in DES and must be co-present when heating begins so the nitrile oxide is immediately trapped. DES is viscous at RT (~50 cP) so the feed line should be gently warmed (40-50°C) to reduce viscosity and ensure consistent pumping.

mixing_order_reasoning: Single stream: all reagents dissolved in DES before entering the heated reactor coil. The nitrile oxide is generated thermally and must be trapped immediately by the dipolarophile to avoid dimerization to furoxan. Pre-mixing ensures the dipolarophile is always present in excess relative to the transient nitrile oxide concentration at any point along the reactor.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds thermally in air without issue.
quench_reasoning: The batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only (extraction). In flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No reactive intermediates persist after the 15 min residence time at 120°C (99% conversion reported).
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide [3+2] cycloaddition). The exact mechanism of nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate in DES is less certain — it likely involves thermal dehydration possibly assisted by TBAB as a mild base, but the DES may play a more complex role. The viscosity of TBAB/EG DES at room temperature (~40-80 cP) is a practical concern for flow pumping; warming the feed reservoir to 40-50°C is recommended. Back-pressure regulator should be used to keep system pressurized above the boiling point concerns at 120°C. Residence time of 15 min at 120°C in a coil reactor is straightforward for flow translation.
